text: For the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system with a smoothed Burgers bore (u_L=1.5, u_R=0, width 0.5) and a KdV-like sech^2 soliton (amplitude 1.5) starting at x=-8 on x in [-15,15], periodic, what numerical scheme integrates stably and accurately to T=8 so we can observe the bore-soliton interaction (transmit/reflect/fuse/destroy), with v retaining a recognizable peak amplitude >= 0.5 and |u_max| < 5?

ic: u0 = 1.5*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore at x=0); v0 = 1.5*sech^2(x+8) (soliton at x=-8)
method: Fourier pseudospectral spatial derivatives (no dealiasing, no filter), explicit RK4 in time. RHS: u_t = -3*u*u_x - 6*v*v_x - v_xxx ; v_t = -6*v*v_x - v_xxx - (u*v)_x. All derivatives via ik in spectral space; nonlinear products pointwise.
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity discipline requires a baseline E1. Bank is NegOnly so cites_bank=[]. I REJECT central-FD baselines for the Burgers term (kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs, kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation) -- they predict Gibbs blow-up. Instead I use Fourier pseudospectral as the single uniform baseline: it handles v_xxx naturally and the bore is smoothed (width 0.5), so spectral may resolve it. NO dealiasing, NO filter, explicit RK4 (no IMEX). The discipline says even if kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup and kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup warn explicit RK4 has stiffness issues for v_xxx, I MUST run it first to anchor the failure mode. dt=1e-4 from linear CFL dt < ~2.8/k_max^3 with k_max~26.8 at Nx=256.

rationale: E1 baseline blew up to NaN/Inf at t=0.5605, well before T=8. Classic signature of v_xxx stiffness combined with un-dealiased aliasing (the bank kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup and kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup forewarned this). The failure mode is OVERFLOW in nonlinear products (u*v, u*v_x), driven by aliasing energy accumulating at the Nyquist scale. Two issues are intertwined: (a) explicit RK4 on v_xxx requires dt ~ dx^3 ~ 1.6e-3 / pi^3 -- tight but at Nx=256, the spectral linear-CFL boundary 2.8/k_max^3 with k_max=26.8 gives dt < 1.45e-4 (we used 1e-4 already), so linear v_xxx is borderline-stable; (b) but the dominant practical failure is aliasing from un-dealiased nonlinear products feeding back into the spectral RHS. The smoothed-tanh bore is largely respected by spectral (width 0.5 is well-resolved by dx=0.117), so Gibbs-from-IC is NOT the primary failure. Localization: linear v_xxx stability is borderline (we hit the CFL boundary at our dt) AND nonlinear aliasing accumulates -- need either (i) implicit treatment of v_xxx, or (ii) dealiasing. Per progressive-complexity discipline E2 may upgrade ONE component.

E1 blew up; per progressive-complexity discipline, change exactly ONE major component. Two candidate single-component upgrades: (A) switch to IMEX-CN treating v_xxx implicitly (bank kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup recommends IMEX-CN as safer default); (B) add 2/3 dealiasing while keeping explicit RK4 (bank kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts says dealiasing essential). Choose (A) IMEX-CN because the bank explicitly identifies the v_xxx stiff term as the primary failure-driver for spectral KdV-class problems (kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup, kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup). Removing the v_xxx stability constraint allows a much larger dt and dramatically reduces the number of explicit nonlinear evaluations per unit T, which by itself reduces aliasing accumulation. NOT going to (B) first because it leaves the explicit v_xxx CFL bottleneck and at A=1.5 the bank kb-gardner-G2 confirms IMEX-CN-explicit-nonlinear is stable. I am avoiding the 'IMEX-CN+dealiasing+lowdt' triple-upgrade trap (would violate the one-component-per-iteration rule).

method: Fourier pseudospectral (no dealiasing -- unchanged from E1) + IMEX-CN. CN treats v_xxx implicitly in both u- and v-equations; nonlinear terms (3uu_x, 6vv_x, (uv)_x) treated explicitly (Euler-on-N). Update: v_hat^{n+1} = (v_hat^n*(1 + a) + dt*Nv_hat)/(1 - a), with a = ik^3 dt/2; then u_hat^{n+1} = u_hat^n + dt*Nu_hat + a*(v_hat^n + v_hat^{n+1}).
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade vs E1: explicit RK4 -> IMEX-CN. Bank kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup explicitly recommends IMEX-CN as 'safer default' over IFRK4 for KdV-class problems; kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup confirms explicit-only treatment of v_xxx fragments solitons even with very small dt. I REJECT IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup) and explicit RK4 with tiny dt (kb-gardner-G1-explicitRK4-finiteFrag: 200k steps still gives 14 spurious peaks). I REJECT amplitude-blind dt choice (kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup: same scheme blew up at A=3.0; A=1.5 needs NL-CFL < ~0.5). At A=1.5: max|6v+1.5v^2| ~ 9+3.375 = 12.4; for u term in coupled system max|3u|=4.5, so combined nonlinear speed ~12.4+4.5=16.9. dt=1e-3 * 16.9 * k_max=26.8 = 0.45 - below safety boundary 0.5-1 from kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary. NO dealiasing yet because progressive-complexity discipline allows only ONE change per iteration; if accuracy is poor we add it at E3.

stderr_warnings: overflow encountered in multiply (in u*v product)

rationale: E2 IMEX-CN at dt=1e-3 (10x larger than E1) blew up at t=0.778, NOT much earlier than E1 (t=0.56) -- this shows the v_xxx CFL was NOT the binding constraint. The blowup originates again in the NONLINEAR-products step: 'overflow encountered in multiply' in u*v. With v_xxx now treated implicitly, the only remaining un-controlled mechanism is aliasing in the un-dealiased nonlinear products. Aliasing energy injects power at high k each step; without dealiasing or a filter, it grows exponentially. At Nx=256, the cubic/quadratic products u*v and v*v_x alias to wavenumbers above N/3, contaminating the spectral RHS. This localizes the remaining failure to ALIASING, exactly the failure mode flagged by kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts and kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing. Per progressive-complexity discipline, E3 may layer ONE more component: 2/3 dealiasing of the nonlinear products.

F1 and F2 together localize the bottleneck: it is ALIASING, not v_xxx stiffness (E2 stiff term was implicit yet still blew up) and not Gibbs-from-IC (the bore is smoothed and well-resolved). Add ONE component on top of E2: 2/3 dealiasing rule on the nonlinear products. Bank entries kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts and kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing both prescribe this exact upgrade as the single missing piece for spectral KdV/Gardner-class solvers. Keep IMEX-CN (the upgrade from E1->E2 was correct in direction even if alone it was insufficient). Keep Nx=256, dt=1e-3.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + IMEX-CN (kept from E2) + 2/3 dealiasing rule on nonlinear products. Inputs (u_hat, v_hat) are 2/3-filtered before forming products; each product (u*u_x, v*v_x, u*v) is then transformed back to spectral and 2/3-filtered again before differentiation in spectral space. Only ONE new component vs E2 (the dealiasing mask). dt=1e-3, T=8.
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade vs E2: add 2/3 dealiasing. Bank kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts (KdV no-dealias produced 4 spurious peaks, 43% amplitude inflation) and kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing (Gardner no-dealias produced 11 spurious peaks) both prescribe this exact upgrade for spectral solvers of KdV/Gardner-class equations -- and the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system has the same quadratic AND cubic-equivalent nonlinearities. F2's blowup at t=0.78 with implicit v_xxx isolates aliasing as the binding constraint. I REJECT switching to Lax-Friedrichs (kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation, kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-overdiffusion warn it over-diffuses bores). I REJECT shrinking dt further as the primary fix (kb-gardner-G1: 200k steps still fragmented). At A=1.5 the NL-CFL at dt=1e-3 is sub-boundary (kb-gardner-G4 amplitude-CFL boundary), so dt is not the issue.

diagnostics.snap2plus_status: all NaN (n_nan=256 for both u and v)
stderr_warnings: overflow encountered in multiply (in nonlinear products u_f*ux_f)

rationale: E3 (IMEX-CN + 2/3 dealiasing) made it 1.9x further than E2 (t=1.474 vs t=0.778) and 2.6x further than E1 (vs t=0.56). The dealiasing upgrade DID help -- but not enough to reach T=8. At t=1.0 (snapshot 1) the physical state is reasonable: max|u|=3.13 (the Burgers bore has steepened from IC max 1.5 by ~2x as 3*u*u_x compresses the descending edge), max v=0.82 (the soliton has propagated and is interacting with the bore region). The blowup at t=1.47 is NOT aliasing-driven anymore (dealiasing handles that) -- it is SHOCK FORMATION in the u-equation. Spectral derivatives applied near a forming shock (where u_x grows without bound) inject energy at the highest resolved (post-dealias) modes; even the 2/3-truncated band cannot represent a discontinuity. This is exactly the failure mode that bank kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs and kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation warn about for ANY non-upwind discretization of nonlinear hyperbolic conservation laws -- and Fourier pseudospectral, despite being beautiful for dispersive terms, is a central-derivative method in disguise. A 4th iteration would adopt a hybrid approach -- e.g. MUSCL or upwind FV for the Burgers u-flux while keeping spectral IMEX for the v-equation v_xxx -- but per progressive-complexity discipline the session iteration cap (3) is now exhausted.

Session iteration cap reached. Final method = E3 (Fourier pseudospectral IMEX-CN + 2/3 dealiasing). Honest self-assessment: the candidate produces physically meaningful results up to t~1.0 then loses stability due to shock formation in the Burgers u-component, which spectral methods cannot resolve without upwind/limited treatment. The phenomenon target (final v amplitude>=0.5 at T=8, |u_max|<5) is NOT MET because the final snapshot is NaN. The trace localizes the next single-component upgrade (replace spectral u-flux with upwind/MUSCL, OR add a strong spectral filter / hyperviscosity to provide artificial dissipation near the shock) but this is left for a future session.
